header.title: Operations SOP
header.subtitle: Truck loading, delivery, cylinder refilling, dealer swapping, and plant receiving - the physical movement of LPG from supplier to client and back.

stages.english: Process Order | Delivery | Return to Plant
sop_steps: Receive the approved Sales Order; client orders 22kg or 50kg cylinder variants. (C1.1.1)
Delivery Head schedules the delivery - vehicle, capacity, route. (C1.2.1)
Warehouse generates the Picklist and picks the LPG/products. (C1.1.2)
Warehouse generates the Delivery Receipt - 2 DRs and 2 Credit Notes per delivery. (C1.1.3)
Warehouse submits the DR to Accounting to post General Ledger and Inventory entries. (C1.1.4.1)
Driver collects the Delivery Receipts from Warehouse (may cover multiple clients on one route). (C1.1.4.2)
Driver and Pahinante proceed to client locations.
For pickup / no-delivery orders, Warehouse gives the DR to the customer for signature instead. (C1.1.4.3)
On site, Pahinante weighs and records the cylinders, then delivers them. (C1.1.5.1)
Client signs the DR, receives the items, and returns the empty cylinders. (C1.1.5.2)
Check the 1-3kg residue threshold; round up or down and classify accordingly. (C1.1.5.3)
Provide Credit Notes to the client, recording cylinder serial number and weight residue. (C1.1.6)
Driver returns to the plant and weighs the empty cylinders. (C1.1.7)
Driver submits delivery records plus the signed DR and AR to Accounting. (C1.1.8)
Accounting reconciles the General Ledger against the signed DR and AR. (C1.1.9)
Compute Commercial Gains - compare weigh-in vs weigh-out records from client site and plant.
Record results in Google Sheets and ACCPAC.
Compute commission and distribute to driver/helper/back-office team.
guidelines: The residue classification threshold is fixed at 1-3kg; deviations are escalated to the Warehouse Supervisor, not decided ad hoc by the driver.
Two DRs and two Credit Notes are mandatory per delivery (client copy + company copy); a delivery without a matching document pair is not closed in the GL.
No cylinder leaves the warehouse without a Picklist reference; no GL/Inventory posting is made without a Warehouse-submitted DR.
Gains and commission are computed only from matched weigh-in/weigh-out pairs - unmatched records are held for investigation, never estimated.

current: Paper DR/CR forms filled by hand at warehouse and client site.
GL reconciled manually against physical DR/AR copies.
Gains re-keyed twice - once to Google Sheets, once to ACCPAC.
Residue judged by the driver/Pahinante at point of delivery.
future: Odoo Sales Order auto-generates Picklist, DR and CR.
Digital client signature capture on delivery.
Automatic weigh-in/weigh-out variance flag and residue computation.
Single-entry GL posting, removing the GSheet/ACCPAC duplicate entry.

stages.sources: Source site (wilsonsykes.github.io/MGSDiagramWorkflow) - Operations > Commercial (C1.1.1-C1.1.9); "MGC LPG Process Manual v2.docx" - Operations > Commercial.

stages.english: Process Order | Delivery | Return to Plant
sop_steps: Receive the booked bulk Sales Order; the order must fill the 8-ton truck capacity.
Assign the lorry/trailer truck and load from the Storage Tank (stock transfer, tank to truck - no cylinders involved).
Weigh the loaded truck at the plant truck scale (weigh-out).
Generate loading documents: Receiving Report, Truck Scale slip, and DR - issued per lorry load, not per cylinder.
Deliver the bulk LPG to the industrial or commercial client site.
At the client site, measure the volume delivered via meter / pressure gauge.
Client receives the bulk LPG and signs the DR.
Truck returns to the plant and is weighed again (weigh-in).
Compute Bulk Gains per truck - compare weigh-out vs weigh-in; withdrawal should read the higher figure.
Record residue in the Commercial & Bulk LPG Residue Summary, per truck.
Submit documents to Accounting; record in Google Sheets and ACCPAC.
guidelines: Bulk orders must fill the 8-ton truck capacity before dispatch is scheduled; a partial load requires Delivery Head sign-off as a documented exception.
Bulk uses truck-scale (plant) + meter/pressure-gauge (client) measurement instead of cylinder weigh-in/out; both readings are logged before the trip is closed.
Per-truck gain is computed only once both weigh-out and weigh-in are on file - a truck missing either reading stays open, it is not estimated.

controls: RR + DR + Truck Scale slip set

current: Truck-scale readings logged by hand on paper slips.
Meter/gauge reading at client site manually copied onto the DR.
Per-truck gain calculated by comparing two paper slips.
future: Odoo Inventory stock-transfer module logs tank-to-truck movement automatically.
Digital truck-scale integration feeding the trip record directly.
Auto-flagged gain/loss variance per truck, no manual comparison.

stages.sources: "MGC LPG Process Manual v2.docx" - Operations > Bulk (adapted from Commercial). Source site (wilsonsykes.github.io/MGSDiagramWorkflow) Operations > Industrial (C2.1-C2.3) was placeholder-only and is superseded by the process manual detail.

stages.english: Process Order | Plant Refilling & Swapping | Invoice
sop_steps: Dealer client orders 2.5kg or 11kg cylinder variants and is scheduled in advance; only confirmed appointments are accommodated. (C3.1)
Sales Rep creates a Sales Order Form noting the delivery/pickup date. (C3.1.2)
Dealer arrives with empty cylinders; guard records the vehicle entry.
Cylinder quantities are counted and the Entry/Exit Form (EEF/EES) is issued; guard validates tank classification (e.g. 11kg, sulit).
Guard radios the Production Team; the checker collects the EEF.
Cylinders are refilled at the refilling area (typically ~8 tons, about 2-3 hours).
Checker encodes the transaction in AppSheet - this is the Refilling Report and the basis of the GRR (General Refilling Report).
Entry Sheet is completed and the LUT is issued by the checker together with the delivery team.
Joseph (Plant Operations Officer) reviews and confirms the transaction and records it in inventory.
Accounting verifies via AppSheet against the official receipt.
Checker verifies the receipt; quantity must match before release.
Dealer proceeds to final inspection; guard performs final exit verification before release. (covers pickup-with-terms, pickup-with-payment, and delivery-with-terms variants, C3.1.2.1-C3.1.2.3)
Cylinder Swapping (allied brands only - Taon Gas, Eco Cylinder, Sulit Gas): dealer submits a swap request via messaging; customer approval is secured.
Cylinder photos are taken and sent to the designated group chat.
Sir RY reviews and approves before the swap proceeds.
Edmond (Warehouse and Quality Supervisor) handles the physical swap and keeps records; dealers must provide their own customer database.
Operations keeps a monthly record of cylinders swapped, plus treatment and disposition.
guidelines: Only confirmed, scheduled appointments are accommodated at the plant - walk-in dealer refills are not processed without a prior booking.
AppSheet entry and the official receipt must match in quantity before a transaction is released; a mismatch holds the release rather than being overridden at the gate.
Cylinder swapping is restricted to approved allied brands only and requires Sir RY's photo-based approval before any physical swap - no swap proceeds on verbal agreement alone.
Dealers must maintain their own customer database on file with Operations for swap traceability.

current: Paper EEF/LUT slips at the gate and refilling area.
Guard radio call to Production to trigger refilling.
AppSheet is semi-digital but still reconciled by hand against paper receipts.
Swap approval runs through ad hoc chat screenshots.
future: One mobile app covers booking -> gate entry -> refill logging -> accounting verification in a single record.
Swap approvals become a structured digital workflow instead of chat threads, with automatic allied-brand validation.

stages.sources: "MGC LPG Process Manual v2.docx" - Operations > Dealer (Plant Refilling & Cylinder Swapping). Source site (wilsonsykes.github.io/MGSDiagramWorkflow) Operations > Dealer (C3.1-C3.3) provided the order-intake framing.

sop_steps: Retail/semi-dealer client orders 11kg or 2.5kg cylinders on a retail scale (smaller quantities than Dealer). (per source site: "same as Dealer steps")
Order intake and refill/delivery fulfilment follow the same plant-refilling flow documented for Dealer, scaled to retail order volumes.
Payment is collected via cash/COD at point of delivery or pickup rather than dealer credit terms - see Accounting > Retail for the cash-handling procedure.
Commission/gains computation follows the same weigh-in/weigh-out and LUT-balancing logic used for Commercial and Dealer.
guidelines: Retail fulfilment is explicitly modeled on the Dealer plant-refilling process per company specification; any retail-specific exception to that process is not yet documented.
Retail is COD by default; credit/terms sales are the exception and require the same past-dues check used in Accounting > Dealer before release.

current: Retail delivery/pickup handled as a scaled-down copy of the Dealer process, with no dedicated documentation on file - inconsistent handling risk across staff.
future: A dedicated Retail SOP variant inside the same Odoo/AppSheet system, with COD-specific checkpoints built in rather than informally inherited from Dealer.

stages.gap_note: Neither source separately documents Operations for Retail - both point to it following the Dealer plant-refilling process, scaled down. Recommend a short walkthrough with a Retail-channel Sales Assistant to confirm whether any retail-specific step (e.g. cash-before-release sequencing) differs from Dealer before this is finalized as approved SOP.
stages.sources: Source site (wilsonsykes.github.io/MGSDiagramWorkflow) Operations > Retail (C4.1-C4.3, "same as Dealer steps"). No corresponding section in "MGC LPG Process Manual v2.docx".

stages.english: Purchase Order | Stock Transfer | Refill | Floor Stock
sop_steps: Purchaser forwards the Purchase Order to the Refinery Supplier and schedules the inbound Lorry Truck delivery. (C5.1.1)
Plant Manager receives the Lorry Trucks and collects the DR and Invoice from the delivery team.
Plant Manager fills out the Receiving Report (RR) and unloads contents to the Storage Tank. (C5.1.2)
Plant Staff measures the content volume using a gauge and documents the reading. (C5.1.3)
Any quantity changes discovered after the RR are reported and logged as a separate entry. (C5.1.3.1)
Stock Transfer: gas is refilled from the Storage Tank to cylinders following FIFO. (C5.1.4)
Refilling is tracked using the Google Sheets/AppSheet tools already in use for cylinder refilling. (C5.1.5)
Storage is split across two locations, Tank 1 and Tank 2; residue is tracked in two categories, Regular and WIWO, both affecting inventory and billing.
Pull-out schedules and dates at client sites are tracked against a company Pull-out Calendar.
Cylinders are purchased annually with individual serial numbers. (C5.3.1)
Cylinders have a defined lifespan requiring repaint and re-testing, tracked by batch number. (C5.3.2)
guidelines: Storage Tank stock transfer to cylinders must follow FIFO; no batch is filled out of sequence without Plant Manager sign-off.
Any quantity variance discovered after the Receiving Report is logged as a separate exception entry, never folded silently into the original RR figure.
Cylinder serials and batch numbers are tracked for their full lifespan (purchase through repaint/retest cycles); a cylinder without a traceable batch number does not re-enter circulation.

approval_matrix.transaction: Storage Tank stock transfer to cylinders

current: Gauge readings and RR logged on paper / Google Sheets.
FIFO sequencing tracked manually by staff log sheets.
Cylinder lifespan tracked in a spreadsheet separate from the refilling records.
future: Digital tank-gauge integration feeding directly into the Odoo Inventory module.
Automatic FIFO enforcement at the point of stock-transfer entry.
A single cylinder-asset register linking serial number, batch, refill history, and lifecycle status.

cross_stage.name: Cross-Stage Governance
cross_stage.description: Escalation and traceability rules that apply on top of every Operations approval row above.
guidelines: The 1-3kg residue threshold for cylinder classification is fixed company policy; a driver or Pahinante does not have discretion to round outside that band without Warehouse Supervisor escalation.
Gains, losses, and commission are computed only from a matched pair of weigh-in and weigh-out readings. An unmatched reading is held for investigation, never estimated.
Every cylinder is tracked by serial number and batch number for its full lifecycle, including repaint/retest - a cylinder without a traceable batch number does not re-enter circulation.
